Name Guides

Baby Names by Theme: Nature, Virtue, Myth, and More

Explore baby names organized by theme — from nature and virtue names to mythological, literary, and celestial-inspired choices.

By Ziven Borceg11 min readFebruary 8, 2026

Looking for a baby name with a specific vibe? This themed guide organizes hundreds of names by category, making it easy to find the perfect name that matches your style.

Nature Names

Botanical

Rose, Lily, Violet, Iris, Dahlia, Jasmine, Poppy, Ivy, Fern, Sage, Juniper, Hazel, Willow, Cedar, Rowan, Magnolia, Azalea, Clover, Briar, Primrose

Celestial

Luna, Stella, Aurora, Nova, Celeste, Orion, Atlas, Phoenix, Vega, Sirius, Callisto, Lyra, Cassiopeia

Water

River, Brook, Marina, Ocean, Coral, Delta, Cove, Bay, Rain, Misty

Earth

Clay, Flint, Terra, Stone, Cliff, Heath, Glen, Dale, Meadow, Prairie

Virtue Names

Historically popular among Puritans, virtue names are seeing a revival:

  • Classic: Grace, Hope, Faith, Joy, Honor, Mercy, Patience
  • Modern Revival: Serenity, Harmony, Journey, Destiny, Trinity
  • Rare Virtues: Verity (truth), Amity (friendship), Felicity (happiness), Clement (mercy)

    • Mythological Names

    Greek

    Athena, Artemis, Apollo, Cassandra, Helen, Penelope, Jason, Theseus, Persephone, Daphne

    Norse

    Freya, Astrid, Ingrid, Thor, Leif, Odin, Sigrid, Brynhild

    Celtic

    Rhiannon, Brigid, Finn, Niamh, Cernunnos, Morrigan

    Literary Names

    From Shakespeare

    Miranda, Cordelia, Juliet, Portia, Rosalind, Viola, Orlando, Sebastian

    From Classic Literature

    Darcy, Atticus, Holden, Heathcliff, Scarlett, Scout, Lyra, Hermione

    From Modern Fiction

    Arya, Katniss, Rue, Peeta, Galadriel, Arwen, Eowyn

    Royal Names

    Elizabeth, Victoria, Catherine, Charlotte, George, Henry, William, Edward, Arthur, Albert

    Check How Many People Have This Name
